attendance-contract.whenToUse: During orientation, when a new student commits to a regular attendance schedule that meets their required weekly/monthly hours.
attendance-contract.whenNotToUse:
- NOT how daily attendance is recorded or hours are proven — that is the [Student Sign-in Sheet](./sign-in-sheet.md).
- NOT attendance proof for the Ready to Work certificate — that is [Ready to Work Attendance Verification](./rtw-attendance.md).

auth-release.whenToUse: When a student authorizes sharing their information with an approved outside agency or person (for example, an employer or a partner organization).
auth-release.whenNotToUse: - NOT for the WV Department of Health Services — same kind of release, but DoHS has its own scoped form: [DoHS Release of Information](./dohs-release.md).

dfa-ts-12.whenToUse: At the end of each month, to compile and submit a WV Works student's total activity hours to DoHS. The daily hours that feed this sheet come from the [Student Sign-in Sheet](./sign-in-sheet.md).
dfa-ts-12.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for documenting daily attendance — that is the [Student Sign-in Sheet](./sign-in-sheet.md).
- NOT for verifying that a student is participating in WV Works — that is the [DFA-WVW-70 Participant Form](./dfa-wvw-70.md).
- NOT for requesting support services such as transportation or childcare — that is the [DFA-WVW-25 Support Services](./dfa-wvw-25.md).

dfa-wvw-25.whenToUse: When a student needs to submit a formal request for WV Works support services — for example, to get reimbursed for transportation costs or to request childcare assistance.
dfa-wvw-25.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for learning about what support services are available — first read the [Support Services Fact Sheet](./support-services-fact-sheet.md), then come back to this form to request them.
- NOT for monthly hours tracking — that is the [DFA-TS-12 Activity Tracking](./dfa-ts-12.md).
- NOT for participation verification — that is the [DFA-WVW-70 Participant Form](./dfa-wvw-70.md).

dfa-wvw-70.whenToUse: When documentation is needed to confirm that a student is actively participating in the WV Works program — for example, when the state or a caseworker requires participation verification.
dfa-wvw-70.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for reporting a student's monthly hours — that is the [DFA-TS-12 Activity Tracking](./dfa-ts-12.md) timesheet.
- NOT for requesting support services — that is the [DFA-WVW-25 Support Services](./dfa-wvw-25.md) form.

dohs-release.whenToUse: When a student authorizes sharing their information specifically with the WV Department of Health Services (DoHS).
dohs-release.whenNotToUse: - NOT for any other recipient. This is the same kind of release as the [Authorization for Release of Information](./auth-release.md) — the only difference is scope: this one is DoHS-only.

dress-code.whenToUse: During orientation, when a student needs to review and acknowledge the professional dress expectations for the classroom and any work-site visits.
dress-code.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for general student rights or program rules — those are covered in [Rights and Responsibilities](./rights-responsibilities.md).
- NOT for technology use rules — that is the [Technology Acceptable Use Policy](./tech-acceptable-use.md).

media-release.whenToUse: During orientation (or whenever needed), when a student is asked whether they consent to having their photo or video used by the program in materials such as newsletters, social media, or grant reports.
media-release.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for sharing student records or personal information with outside agencies — that is the [Authorization for Release of Information](./auth-release.md) or [DoHS Release of Information](./dohs-release.md).
- NOT a general information-release form — this is specifically about images and video.

non-discrimination.whenToUse: During orientation, when a student must be given the required federal notice of their equal-opportunity rights in the program.
non-discrimination.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for general program rules or student expectations — those are in [Rights and Responsibilities](./rights-responsibilities.md).
- NOT a release-of-information form — for information sharing see [Authorization for Release of Information](./auth-release.md) or [DoHS Release of Information](./dohs-release.md).

portfolio-checklist-tracking.whenToUse: When a student (or instructor) needs to track progress toward completing the employment portfolio — checking off items such as resume, certifications, and work samples as they are completed over time.
portfolio-checklist-tracking.whenNotToUse: - NOT for the initial orientation overview of portfolio requirements — see [Employment Portfolio Checklist](./portfolio-checklist.md) for the onboarding introduction to what a portfolio requires.

portfolio-checklist.whenToUse: During orientation, when a student needs to understand what is required to complete their employment portfolio — the full list of what they will need to collect and build.
portfolio-checklist.whenNotToUse: - NOT for ongoing progress tracking as portfolio items are completed — use [Employment Portfolio Checklist (Tracking)](./portfolio-checklist-tracking.md) for that ongoing check-off.

rights-responsibilities.whenToUse: During orientation, when a new student needs to read and acknowledge their rights within the program and understand what is expected of them.
rights-responsibilities.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for reviewing equal-opportunity or federal non-discrimination rules — that is the [Non-Discrimination Notice](./non-discrimination.md).
- NOT for the attendance commitment specifically — that is the [Personal Attendance Contract](./attendance-contract.md).

rtw-attendance.whenToUse: When a student is completing the Ready to Work certification process and needs their attendance record verified to satisfy the certificate requirement.
rtw-attendance.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for recording daily classroom attendance — that is the [Student Sign-in Sheet](./sign-in-sheet.md).
- NOT the student's one-time attendance commitment made at orientation — that is the [Personal Attendance Contract](./attendance-contract.md).
- NOT for verifying WV Works participation hours on the state timesheet — that is [DFA-TS-12 Activity Tracking](./dfa-ts-12.md).

sign-in-sheet.whenToUse: Every class day, to record who attended. This is the record an instructor uses to total each student's hours for the monthly timesheet.
sign-in-sheet.whenNotToUse:
- NOT the one-time commitment to attend — that is the [Personal Attendance Contract](./attendance-contract.md).
- NOT the monthly state timesheet itself — daily hours are totaled onto [DFA-TS-12 Activity Tracking](./dfa-ts-12.md).
- NOT attendance proof for the Ready to Work certificate — that is [Ready to Work Attendance Verification](./rtw-attendance.md).

student-profile.whenToUse: During orientation, when a new student is enrolling and the program needs to collect their personal background and contact information for the record.
student-profile.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for recording rights or program expectations — that is the [Rights and Responsibilities](./rights-responsibilities.md) form.
- NOT a general orientation checklist — see the [SPOKES Orientation Checklist](../documents/spokes-checklist-for-student-orientation.md) for the full intake sequence.

support-services-fact-sheet.whenToUse: When a student or instructor needs to understand what WV Works support services are available (transportation, childcare, work-related costs, etc.) before submitting a request.
support-services-fact-sheet.whenNotToUse: - NOT the form to actually request support services — this fact sheet explains what exists; to make a request use the [DFA-WVW-25 Support Services](./dfa-wvw-25.md) form.

tech-acceptable-use.whenToUse: During orientation, when a student needs to read and sign the rules for appropriate use of program computers and internet access before they begin using technology resources.
tech-acceptable-use.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for general program rules or conduct — those are in [Rights and Responsibilities](./rights-responsibilities.md).
- NOT for dress or appearance standards — that is the [Dress Code Policy](./dress-code.md).

welcome-letter.whenToUse: At the very start of orientation, when a new student is first joining the program and needs an encouraging introduction to what SPOKES is and what they can expect.
welcome-letter.whenNotToUse:
- NOT for detailed orientation steps or required form signatures — use the [SPOKES Orientation Checklist](../documents/spokes-checklist-for-student-orientation.md) to guide the full intake process.
- NOT for program rules or expectations — those are covered in [Rights and Responsibilities](./rights-responsibilities.md).
